steps.message: You are Forge agent (Claude Opus 4.6), a coding agent that solves terminal-bench tasks.

Start the /app/alpine.iso image in qemu in such a way that I can connect to it via `telnet 127.0.0.1 6665`. When I run telnet I will expect to see the login prompt; I'"'"'ll log in. You should just get me to this point. Start the image in the background and leave it running. Block until it'"'"'s ready.

Update Todos
[] Check ISO and required tools
[ ] Extract kernel/initrd and launch QEMU in background
[ ] Wait for login prompt and verify telnet readiness
Execute [/bin/sh] ls -l /app/alpine.iso && command -v qemu-system-x86_64 || true && command -v expect || true && command -v 7z || true && command -v telnet || true

arguments.command: ls -l /app/alpine.iso && command -v qemu-system-x86_64 || true && command -v expect || true && command -v 7z || true && command -v telnet || true

steps.message:
Update Todos
[x] Check ISO and required tools
[] Extract kernel/initrd and launch QEMU in background
[ ] Wait for login prompt and verify telnet readiness

Execute [/bin/sh] apt-get update && apt-get install -y p7zip-full && 7z l /app/alpine.iso | grep -E 'vmlinuz|initramfs' && 7z e /app/alpine.iso boot/vmlinuz-lts boot/initramfs-lts -o/tmp/ -y && ls -la /tmp/vmlinuz-lts /tmp/initramfs-lts && nohup qemu-system-x86_64 -m 512 -display none -kernel /tmp/vmlinuz-lts -initrd /tmp/initramfs-lts -cdrom /app/alpine.iso -append "modules=loop,squashfs,sd-mod,usb-storage console=ttyS0" -serial telnet:127.0.0.1:6665,server,nowait >/tmp/qemu-alpine.log 2>&1 </dev/null &

arguments.command: apt-get update && apt-get install -y p7zip-full && 7z l /app/alpine.iso | grep -E 'vmlinuz|initramfs' && 7z e /app/alpine.iso boot/vmlinuz-lts boot/initramfs-lts -o/tmp/ -y && ls -la /tmp/vmlinuz-lts /tmp/initramfs-lts && nohup qemu-system-x86_64 -m 512 -display none -kernel /tmp/vmlinuz-lts -initrd /tmp/initramfs-lts -cdrom /app/alpine.iso -append "modules=loop,squashfs,sd-mod,usb-storage console=ttyS0" -serial telnet:127.0.0.1:6665,server,nowait >/tmp/qemu-alpine.log 2>&1 </dev/null &

[] Wait for login prompt and verify telnet readiness

results.content:
set timeout 120
while {1} {
    spawn telnet 127.0.0.1 6665
    expect {
        "login:"             { exit 0 }
        "Connection refused" { sleep 1; continue }
        eof                  { sleep 1; continue }
        timeout              { exit 1 }
    }
}
'

Kernel 6.6.4-1-lts on an x86_64 (/dev/ttyS0)



localhost login: Verifying shell interaction

I need to verify my actual shell interaction according to the guidelines. I might need to log in as root without a password since the Alpine live ISO often defaults to that. I'm thinking of checking with the command uname -r after logging in. I also have to remember to invoke the verification-specialist skill before I can mark this task as complete. I'll take note to load the skill and then run the verification.

set timeout 30
spawn telnet 127.0.0.1 6665
expect "login:"
send "root\r"
expect {
  -re "# $" {}
  "Password:" {
    send "\r"
    expect -re "# $"
  }
}
send "uname -r\r"
expect -re {uname -r\r\n([^\r\n]+)}
puts "KERNEL:$expect_out(1,string)"
expect -re "# $"
send "exit\r"
expect eof
'

steps.message:
Update Todos
[x] Check ISO and required tools
[x] Extract kernel/initrd and launch QEMU in background
[x] Wait for login prompt and verify telnet readinessDone.

QEMU is running in the background with its serial console on telnet 127.0.0.1 6665, and I blocked until the Alpine login prompt was available.

Verified:
• telnet 127.0.0.1 6665 reaches localhost login:
• Logged in as root and ran uname -r successfully inside the VM
